decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Previous iterations focusing on single variables have stalled. Following the optimization history suggestion, I will execute a coordinated multi-variable change targeting the heat spreader's geometry and material properties to maximize lateral heat spreading and conduction path efficiency.

changes.reason: Maximize spreader width to the domain boundary (1.2m limit allows up to 1.0m from x0=0.25) to increase the area available for lateral heat dissipation towards the cold boundaries.
Increase spreader height by 50% (within 1.5x limit) to reduce vertical thermal resistance between the chip and the bulk of the spreader material.
No change; conductivity is already at the upper bound. The strategy shifts to maximizing geometric cross-section since material tuning has hit its limit.

expected_effects: Significant reduction in chip peak temperature due to improved lateral spreading area and reduced vertical resistance.
Temperature margin should improve from -4.26°C to a feasible positive value or close to zero.
risk_notes: Increasing spreader height reduces the gap between the spreader and the top boundary; ensure no overlap occurs with other potential components (none currently defined above).
Geometric changes may alter mesh quality if the aspect ratio becomes extreme, though the proposed dimensions remain within reasonable bounds.
